FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE  
   
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
   
1Q13 
 
 
Financial Highlights: 
  
First Quarter 2013 
 
Net revenues were NT$132.76 billion, representing a 1.0% increase 
from NT$131.45 billion in 4Q12 and a 25.7% increase from 
NT$105.62 billion in 1Q12. 
 
Gross margin was 45.8%, down 1.4 percentage points from 4Q12 and 
down 2.0 percentage points from 1Q12.  
 
Operating margin was 33.5%, down 1.8 percentage points from 4Q12 
and up 0.2 percentage point from 1Q12. 
 
Non-operating income and long-term investment combined were a gain 
of NT$1.32 billion, compared to a loss of NT$0.11 billion in 4Q12 and 
a gain of NT$0.56 billion in 1Q12. 
 
Net income attributable to shareholders of the parent company was 
NT$39.58 billion, down 4.9% from 4Q12 and up 18.2% from 1Q12.  
Net profit margin was 29.8% and diluted EPS was NT$1.53. 
 
Special Remark 
1. 
Starting 2013, TSMC prepares financial statements in accordance with 
TIFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards as endorsed in 
R.O.C.). TSMC no longer provides R.O.C. GAAP-based financial 
statements. 
2. 
All amounts in this report are based on TIFRS. An illustration of the 
major differences between 2012 financial statements under TIFRS and 
under R.O.C. GAAP is available separately in the report of "Major 
Differences between TIFRS and R.O.C. GAAP for TSMC". 
(Amounts are on consolidated basis and are in NT
billions unless otherwise noted)

Page 2 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Revenue Analysis: 
In the first quarter, demand for TSMC’s wafers was 
stronger than seasonal as mobile customers accelerated 
their new product launches for the year. By 
application, 
Communication, 
Computer, 
and 
Consumer, increased 4%, 3%, and 3% from 4Q12, 
respectively, while Industrial/Standard decreased 2% 
sequentially. 
28nm process technology contributed 24% of total 
wafer revenues for the quarter. Advanced technologies 
(40/45nm and below) accounted for 47% of total wafer 
revenues, up from 44% in 4Q12 and 37% in 1Q12.   
Revenues from Fabless/System customers accounted 
for 87% of total wafer revenues in 1Q13. 
From a geographic perspective, revenues from 
customers based in North America accounted for 69% 
of total wafer revenues, while revenues from Asia 
Pacific, China, Europe and Japan accounted for 14%, 
7%, 8%, and 2% of total wafer revenues, respectively. 
 
I.  Revenue Analysis 
II. Profit & Expense Analysis 
Gross Profit Analysis: 
Gross margin was 45.8% in 1Q13, 1.4 percentage 
points lower than 4Q12, mainly due to higher capacity 
utilization and a favorable foreign exchange rate, more 
than offset by unfavorable inventory valuation 
adjustments due to changes in utilization rates.

Non-Operating Items: 
Total non-operating items were a gain of NT$1.32 
billion in 1Q13. 
Net investment gain was NT$0.65 billion, down 
NT$0.09 billion sequentially, reflecting lower earnings 
from certain invested companies.  
Interest expenses were NT$0.49 billion, up NT$0.15 
billion from 4Q12, mainly attributed to the issuance of 
corporate bonds in 1Q13. 
Other gains and losses were NT$1.16 billion, up 
NT$1.67 billion from 4Q12, mostly resulted from the 
absence of impairment losses from certain invested 
companies in 4Q12. 
Operating Income Analysis: 
Total operating expenses increased NT$0.69 billion to 
NT$16.38 billion in 1Q13, primarily reflecting higher 
opening expenses for Fab15 capacity expansion. 
Total operating expenses represented 12.3% of 
revenue in 1Q13. 
III. Financial Condition Review 
Liquidity Analysis: 
At the end of 1Q13, cash and marketable securities 
totaled NT$189.26 billion, increased by NT$38.34 
billion from 4Q12, primarily due to the proceeds of 
NT$45.00 billion from issuance of corporate bonds 
and an increase of NT$1.12 billion in short-term loans, 
partially offset by a negative free cash flow of 
NT$6.85 billion during the quarter (please refer to 
page 5). 
Total current liabilities increased by NT$9.73 billion, 
as income tax payable increased NT$4.53 billion and 
payable to equipment suppliers increased NT$3.77 
billion during the quarter.   
Net working capital was NT$139.54 billion and 
current ratio was 1.9x.

Debt Service: 
 
Net cash reserves, defined as cash and short-term 
marketable securities minus interest-bearing debts, 
decreased NT$7.80 billion to NT$25.34 billion in 
1Q13, primarily reflecting proceeds of NT$45.00 
billion from the issuance of corporate bonds and an 
increase of NT$1.12 billion in short-term loans, offset 
by an increase of NT$38.34 billion in cash and 
marketable securities. 
Receivable and Inventory Days: 
 
Days of receivable remained at 43 days, and days of 
inventory increased by one day to 51 days. 
 
IV. Cash Flow  
Summary of Cash Flow: 
 
Cash generated from operating activities totaled 
NT$73.57 billion during the quarter, a decrease of 
NT$11.23 billion from 4Q12, primarily attributed to 
the increase in other operating uses. The increase in 
other operating uses mainly reflected the changes in 
accounts receivable, inventory, and accounts payable. 
 
Net cash used in investing activities decreased 
NT$12.74 billion to NT$77.03 billion in 1Q13, 
primarily reflecting the absence of acquisition of 
ASML shares NT$31.52 billion in 4Q12, partially 
offset by the increase of NT$20.65 billion in capital 
expenditures.  
 
Net cash generated from financing activities totaled   
NT$44.94 billion during the quarter, mainly reflecting 
the proceeds from issuance of corporate bonds.  
 
At the end of 1Q13, TSMC’s cash balance was 
NT$186.03 billion.

Capacity: 
Total capacity decreased 1.1% to 3,883K 8-inch 
equivalent wafers in 1Q13, primarily due to fewer 
working days and scheduled annual maintenance. 
Total capacity in 2Q13 is expected to increase 3.0% 
sequentially to 3,999K 8-inch equivalent wafers, 
mainly attributed to capacity increase in 28nm process 
technology. 
On a full year basis, total capacity is expected to 
increase 11% from 14,833K 8-inch equivalent wafers 
in 2012 to 16,454K in 2013, in which 12-inch wafer 
capacity is expected to increase 17% year-over-year. 
 
Operating and Free Cash Flows: 
 
Free cash flow, defined as the excess of operating cash 
flows over capital expenditures, was negative NT$6.85 
billion in 1Q13, decreased by NT$31.88 billion from 
4Q12, mainly due to lower operating cash flow and 
higher capital expenditures.
